title: After-Action Report — the finish is a reliever credit (not the starter/winner)
scope: `o27v2/sim.py` (terminal-out / quality-finish accrual), `o27v2/web/box_score.py` (`pick_finisher`), `o27v2/web/app.py` (the two finisher call sites).

rationale.item: The box-score F: line was credited to whichever pitcher on the winning team had the most terminal_outs — with no starter filter, no win-pitcher filter, and no minimum-outs floor. The code even documented it: "a finisher can also be the winning pitcher."

terminal_outs accrue for any spell that entered with a lead, never let it slip, and finished — which a complete-game starter does for ~27 outs. So the starter got tagged the finisher, producing the nonsensical line the owner caught: a single game scored as a complete game + win + finish.
Why both layers: the sim.py change fixes the persisted counting stat going forward; the app.py/box_score change fixes the designation (the F: line) everywhere immediately, including historical games.

delegation_events.decision: The intended finisher rule (reliever-only, 4+ terminal outs, lead maintained, not starter nor decision-taker; no finish if nobody qualifies)
delegation_events.quote: The intended rule (owner spec): A finish is a **reliever** credit.

actions.description: terminal_outs and quality_finish no longer accrue to the starter (... and not is_starter); lead_entries/lead_held (LR%) unchanged
pick_finisher(win_rows, win_pid=None, min_outs=FINISH_MIN_OUTS=4) skips any is_starter row and the win_pid, requires terminal_outs >= 4, returns None when no reliever qualifies

Both finisher call sites (_game_finisher and the batch box-score decorator) now select is_starter and compute the win pitcher via credit_win, passing win_pid to pick_finisher — corrects the F: line on already-simmed DBs without a re-sim

risk_posture: (`lead_entries` / `lead_held`, which drive LR%, are unchanged — lead retention legitimately includes starters.)
This corrects the F: line on **already-simmed** DBs without a re-sim, since the exclusions are applied at display time.

validation_claims.claim: After a fresh sim, starters have 0 terminal_outs and 0 quality_finish; relievers accrue normally.
validation_claims.check_method: Fresh sim and query game_pitcher_stats by is_starter

validation_claims.claim: A complete-game win shows no finisher (finisher=None).
validation_claims.check_method: Find a CG win and check the F: line

validation_claims.claim: Across 30 played games: 7 finishers credited, 0 violations — none is the starter, none is the winning pitcher, every one has 4+ terminal outs.
validation_claims.check_method: Sim 30 games and audit finisher designations

validation_claims.claim: Stat-invariant suite + test_rotation pass; o27v2/tests 129 passed; the box score renders 200.
validation_claims.check_method: Run pytest and render a box score

non_actions.item: One-off historical recompute of persisted terminal_outs / quality_finish counting totals for pre-fix games
non_actions.reason: it's not required for correctness going forward

residual_risks: The season-to-date parenthetical on the F: line (e.g. "F: Brockman (33)") can carry old inflation for historical games — even though the per-game designation is now correct everywhere. New sims are clean.
open_hooks: A one-off historical recompute of those two columns (re-derive per spell with the not is_starter rule) would retroactively correct the résumé totals if desired.
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — the finish is a reliever credit (not the starter/winner)
unmapped_content:
**Branch:** `claude/war-oaa-reconciliation-koalas-7t0pG`
**Commit:** `be0e323`

Root-cause table (section 3): sim.py terminal-out accrual credited terminal_outs (and quality_finish) to any finishing lead-held spell, starters included — a starter finishing his own start isn't a "finish"; box_score.pick_finisher returned the winning team's max(terminal_outs) pitcher, full stop — no starter / win-pitcher / 4-out filter; app.py finisher call sites called pick_finisher(win_rows) with no win-pitcher context — couldn't exclude the decision-taker.

Owner-spec detail: finished the game (recorded the last out), with 4 or more terminal outs, entered with a lead and never let it be tied or lost, and is neither the starter NOR the decision-taking pitcher (the win / majority-relief arm). If nobody qualifies — a complete game, or a game where the closer also took the win — no finish is credited. A complete game / a win is its own credit and is never also a finish. The same flaw tagged non-CG starters who led and finished, and let the winning pitcher double as the finisher.
